claim_boundary.next_gate: Replace transferred mappings with a circuit-family-agnostic deterministic mapping rule and require zero automatic-baseline losses on a new holdout.
claim_boundary.what_is_not_supported: Verifier acceptance, causal hardware performance, readout mitigation, current calibration, provider access, hardware execution, protocol soundness, quantum advantage, BQP separation, or new B10 credit.
claim_boundary.what_is_supported: Unseen circuit-family compiler evidence that the R132 route remains deterministic but fails the automatic-layout no-loss criterion, with mapping-versus-policy attribution.

requirements.label: R132 source and selected policy are hash-bound
four unseen source circuit families are materialized
fresh holdout seeds are disjoint and no holdout selection occurs
all 12 groups retain one route-exposure class
all 12 groups retain one exact QASM hash across seeds
all 120 constrained circuits replay in a fresh process
all holdout rows compare constrained, fixed-map, and automatic routes
every automatic-baseline loss has a mapping-or-policy attribution
verifier acceptance, mitigation, calibration, and hardware remain excluded
no soundness, advantage, BQP, or new credit is claimed
